author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Access-denied path violates the 403 criterion: with 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output', menu_execute_active_handler() passes MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (integer 3) directly to the delivery callback when access fails, and drupal_json_output() neither sets a Status header nor handles menu status codes — anonymous users get HTTP 200 with JSON body '3' instead of Drupal's 403 access-denied response (only drupal_deliver_html_page() translates MENU_ACCESS_DENIED into a 403).

A correct D7 implementation needs a small custom delivery callback that delegates integer menu status codes to drupal_deliver_html_page() and only routes array results through drupal_json_output(); the success path (permission, query logic, JSON shape, Content-Type) is otherwise fine but does not offset the broken 403 behavior.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Registering 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' in hook_menu() replaces drupal_deliver_html_page(), which is the function that translates MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (403) and MENU_NOT_FOUND (404) page-callback results into an actual HTTP 'Status: 403 Forbidden' header and themed error page.

drupal_json_output() has no special-case handling for integer results like MENU_ACCESS_DENIED — on access denied it will just JSON-encode the integer (echoing '403') with a 200 OK status, so anonymous/unpermissioned users get HTTP 200 instead of the required HTTP 403, violating the access-control acceptance criterion.

A correct implementation needs the custom delivery callback to detect integer page-callback results (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED/MENU_NOT_FOUND) and fall back to drupal_deliver_html_page($page_callback_result), which this submission does not do.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: ✓ GET /api/healthstats returns correct JSON shape {"users": int, "nodes": int}
✓ Queries correctly count active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1) using parameterized db_query
✓ Permission 'view healthstats' properly defined via hook_permission()
✓ Menu item correctly specifies 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'), which defaults to user_access() callback in D7 — anonymous users without permission get 403
✓ JSON delivery via 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' is D7's native mechanism; sets correct Content-Type header
✓ No print/exit used; output delegated to delivery callback
✓ Syntax valid; follows Drupal coding standards (2-space indentation, doc comments on hooks, proper hook naming)
✓ All acceptance criteria satisfied

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: hook_permission correctly defines 'view healthstats' with title/description
hook_menu registers api/healthstats with access arguments enforcing the permission, so anonymous users get Drupal's 403
Page callback counts users.status=1 and node.status=1 with placeholders and casts to int, matching the required JSON shape
Delivery via drupal_json_output in a custom delivery callback is D7-native (no print+exit) and sets application/json

Delivery callback correctly hands integer menu status codes (e.g. MENU_ACCESS_DENIED) to drupal_deliver_html_page, so access denied yields a real 403 instead of JSON-encoded '3' with HTTP 200 — the classic custom-delivery-callback pitfall is avoided
Syntax is valid and doc comments/indentation follow Drupal coding standards

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: hook_permission() correctly defines 'view healthstats' with title/description via t()
hook_menu() sets 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats') which defaults to the 'user_access' access callback, correctly gating the path so anonymous/unauthorized users get MENU_ACCESS_DENIED
Page callback queries {users} and {node} WHERE status = 1 exactly as specified and casts results to (int), producing {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>}

Custom 'delivery callback' correctly distinguishes the page callback's array result (delivered via drupal_json_output(), setting Content-Type: application/json) from integer menu status codes (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED/MENU_NOT_FOUND), which it forwards to drupal_deliver_html_page() so Drupal emits a proper 403 instead of JSON-encoding the status int with a 200 — this is the correct idiomatic D7 pattern for overriding delivery while preserving access-denied semantics
No print+exit; uses D7's native drupal_deliver_page()/delivery-callback mechanism throughout
Code is syntactically valid, uses array() syntax, 'Implements hook_X().' doc comments, and consistent indentation per D7 coding standards

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Permission 'view healthstats' is properly defined via hook_permission with correct title/description
Menu item at 'api/healthstats' correctly specifies access arguments; access check will run via default user_access callback
Page callback correctly queries active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1) using parameterized queries
Return array has correct structure: {"users": int, "nodes": int}
Delivery callback uses drupal_json_output() - D7's native JSON mechanism with proper Content-Type
Delivery callback correctly handles menu status codes (access denied etc.) by delegating to drupal_deliver_html_page() for 403 responses
Code passes php -l syntax check, follows D7 coding standards (2-space indentation, proper hook docblocks)
Uses D7 APIs throughout: db_query with parameterized placeholders, hook system, menu system, drupal_json_output

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: 403 criterion violated: registering drupal_json_output as the menu item's delivery callback means an access-denied request delivers MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (int 3) through drupal_json_output, producing HTTP 200 with body '3' instead of Drupal's access denied page

drupal_json_output does not handle menu status codes (unlike drupal_deliver_html_page); the correct D7 pattern is a custom delivery callback that delegates integer results to drupal_deliver_html_page, or calling drupal_json_output() inside the page callback and returning nothing

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: The users count query (`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {users} WHERE status = :status` with status=1) does not exclude uid 0, Drupal's built-in anonymous user row, which defaults to status = 1 in the {users} table — this inflates the reported 'users' count by one and does not accurately reflect active user accounts, a classic D7 gotcha a senior reviewer must catch (should filter `AND uid > 0`).

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Route and JSON shape: GET /api/healthstats correctly returns {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>} via healthstats_stats() callback with explicit (int) casts
Active users/published nodes: Queries correctly filter on status = 1 for both {users} and {node} tables

Permission enforcement: hook_permission() defines 'view healthstats'; hook_menu() specifies access_arguments without explicit access_callback, correctly defaulting to user_access() which will deny anonymous users (403)
JSON delivery: Uses drupal_json_output as delivery callback, D7's native JSON delivery mechanism with correct Content-Type; no print/exit
Syntax and standards: Parameterized queries prevent SQL injection; doc comments on all hooks; consistent 2-space indentation; should pass php -l

hook_permission properly defines 'view healthstats', so anonymous users without it get access denied

the delivery callback correctly hands integer menu codes (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED etc.) to drupal_deliver_html_page, so denied requests return a real HTTP 403 instead of the status code being JSON-encoded with a 200 — the classic trap here is avoided
counts query {users} and {node} with status = 1, cast to int, producing exactly {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>}

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: AC1: counts active users (users.status=1) and published nodes (node.status=1) with parameterized db_query, returns array('users'=>int,'nodes'=>int) -> {"users":N,"nodes":M} exactly
AC2: 'view healthstats' defined in hook_permission and enforced via access arguments; anon without it yields MENU_ACCESS_DENIED
AC2 edge: delivery callback guards is_int() and hands menu status codes to drupal_deliver_html_page, so the 403/404 path still renders correctly instead of JSON-encoding -1 with a 200
AC3: native delivery callback + drupal_json_output (Content-Type application/json), followed by drupal_page_footer(); no print/exit
AC4: valid PHP, correct D7 hook_menu keys ('page callback'/'access arguments'/'delivery callback'), 2-space indent, doc comments on every hook

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: GET /api/healthstats returns exactly {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>} — both COUNT(*) queries are explicitly cast with (int), avoiding the common bug of leaving fetchField()'s string return type (which would serialize as JSON strings, not numbers)

Access is gated by a new 'view healthstats' permission defined via hook_permission and wired through 'access arguments' in hook_menu (default access callback user_access); users lacking it get Drupal's real access-denied response

Delivery uses a proper 'delivery callback' (D7's native mechanism, same pattern core uses for AJAX endpoints) — not print+exit — and correctly delegates integer menu status codes (e.g. MENU_ACCESS_DENIED) to drupal_deliver_html_page so the 403 status header and access-denied page are correctly emitted; success responses go through drupal_json_output() which sets Content-Type: application/json
php -l would pass (balanced braces/parens, valid syntax); 2-space indentation and full doc comments on hook_menu/hook_permission match Drupal coding standards
Queries correctly target status = 1 on {users} and {node} tables per spec, with no scope creep (no caching/paging/extra endpoints, healthstats.info untouched)

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: hook_menu() correctly defines the endpoint with 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'); Drupal 7 defaults to 'access callback' => 'user_access' when unspecified, so this permits access only to users with the permission
hook_permission() defines the required 'view healthstats' permission with proper title and description
Access denial (FALSE from user_access()) causes the menu system to return MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (0) to the delivery callback, which then calls drupal_deliver_html_page(0), resulting in HTTP 403
healthstats_stats_page() queries for active users (status = 1) and published nodes (status = 1), returns array with exact keys 'users' and 'nodes' as integers
healthstats_json_deliver() uses drupal_json_output() (D7 native) to output JSON with correct Content-Type, never uses print/exit
Delivery callback correctly handles integer menu-status codes by delegating to drupal_deliver_html_page(), and normal results via drupal_json_output()
db_query() uses parameterized :status placeholder syntax, safe from SQL injection
Code is syntactically valid: matching braces, correct array syntax, proper indentation (2 spaces), doc comments on all hooks and functions following Drupal standards

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: hook_menu + hook_permission correctly define api/healthstats guarded by 'view healthstats'; anonymous access yields MENU_ACCESS_DENIED, which the delivery callback hands to drupal_deliver_html_page for a proper HTTP 403
delivery callback pattern mirrors D7 core's ajax_deliver(): drupal_json_output() sets Content-Type application/json and drupal_page_footer() performs a clean shutdown — no print+exit
counts query {users}/{node} with status = 1 and cast to int, so the payload is exactly {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>}
syntax is valid and code follows D7 standards (doc comments on hooks, array() syntax, untranslated menu title, placeholders in db_query)

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: JSON shape {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>} correctly produced via db_query COUNT with status=1 filters and (int) casts
hook_permission defines 'view healthstats'; hook_menu uses default 'user_access' access callback via 'access arguments', correctly gating anonymous users

Custom delivery callback correctly detects integer menu status codes (e.g. MENU_ACCESS_DENIED) and falls back to drupal_deliver_html_page, so access-denied still yields Drupal's native 403 HTML response instead of JSON-encoding the integer — this is the classic D7 delivery-callback trap and it's handled correctly, matching core's own ajax_deliver() pattern
Success path uses drupal_json_output() (correct Content-Type: application/json) followed by drupal_page_footer(), Drupal's native JSON delivery convention, not print+exit
Code is syntactically valid, uses D7 array() syntax, proper hook doc comments, consistent 2-space indentation — passes coding standards and php -l

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: Missing `access callback` key in hook_menu(). In Drupal 7, omitting this key causes all access to be granted to all users regardless of permissions. The `access arguments` array alone cannot enforce access control without an explicit callback function. Standard pattern requires: 'access callback' => 'user_access', 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats')

Acceptance criterion violated: 'Access requires a new permission `view healthstats`; anonymous users without it get Drupal's access denied (HTTP 403).' Without the access callback, anonymous users WILL have access to the endpoint instead of receiving 403.

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: hook_menu with page callback + 'delivery callback' => drupal_json_output is D7's native JSON delivery with correct Content-Type, no print/exit

'view healthstats' defined in hook_permission and enforced via access arguments; menu status ints (403) are correctly delegated to drupal_deliver_html_page so anonymous users get a proper access denied
queries count active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1) with placeholders and {table} prefixes; output shape is exactly {"users": int, "nodes": int} with (int) casts
syntax is valid and code follows Drupal coding standards (doc blocks on hooks, 2-space indentation, t() on permission strings)

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: healthstats_deliver_json() manually calls drupal_page_footer() in the success/JSON branch, but drupal_deliver_page() (the core function that invokes any custom 'delivery callback') already calls drupal_page_footer() itself immediately after the delivery callback returns — so on every successful request hook_exit and other footer processing (cache writes, cron trigger) run twice.

The bug is inconsistently applied: the int/error branch correctly omits the extra call (relying on the framework's own footer invocation), while the JSON branch adds a redundant one — showing incorrect understanding of D7's page delivery lifecycle rather than a deliberate design choice.
This is a genuine production defect in how the module integrates with D7's native delivery mechanism, even though the JSON body/content-type themselves are correct.

author: claude-fable-5

hook_menu() correctly registers 'api/healthstats' with access control via default user_access() callback with 'view healthstats' permission argument

Delivery callback uses drupal_json_output() (native D7 JSON function) instead of print+exit, with correct Content-Type handling
Delivery callback properly handles menu status constants (access denied returns -2, delegated to drupal_deliver_html_page() for 403 response)
Syntax valid, Drupal coding standards followed (hook documentation, indentation, use of t() for strings, parameterized queries)
